body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Ground Loop plugin-host supervisor
body.intention: I intend that predicted unintended effects of the Ground Loop are checked within two weeks of shipping so any silent regressions are caught before they compound.
body.description:
**What changed:** Ground Loop plugin-host daemon added below the rack stack; web_server managed via http_proxy plugin; CC recovery consolidated into on_failure hook.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
(1) Proxy latency — every web_server request now passes through Ground Loop proxy; if proxy adds >50ms on the hot path that degrades UX silently.
(2) Double-start race — if Ground Loop and another launcher both start web_server within the same poll window, two backend instances conflict on the port.
(3) CC recovery loop — if Igor fails to start repeatedly, Ground Loop fires CC recovery repeatedly; CC fixes it, Igor starts, fails again (different bug), loop continues burning tokens.
(4) systemd masking — if Ground Loop is a systemd unit and the user manually starts it too, two instances run; need pid lock or systemd ConditionPathExists guard.
(5) HTTPS CONNECT tunnel — if any client sends HTTPS to the proxy and the tunnel handling is incomplete, requests silently drop instead of erroring clearly.
(6) Plugin YAML parse errors — a malformed YAML in the plugin dir must not crash the loop; fail-open is the rule but needs verification.

**Signals to watch:** web_server response time before vs after (curl --write-out timings); Ground Loop logs for double-spawn events; CC invocation count in budget logs after any Igor instability period; proxy error rate in Ground Loop logs.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-27 or after first week of Ground Loop running in production.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** All 6 predicted effects checked; outcome annotated; ticket closed with one-line verdict.
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
